comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Realigned to the SHIPPED workflow engine (epic e6d9, CLOSED: src/rebar/llm/workflow/ — declarative IR + interpreter/executor + run_workflow CLI/MCP + PydanticAI runner) and the SHIPPED contract-bearing prompt model (epic da27, closed this session: src/rebar/llm/prompts.py — front-matter inputs/outputs contracts, execution_mode, derived index, .rebar/prompts override). 'Reuse rebar.llm extension points' is now concrete machinery. The residual open dependency is 6f2d (gate-as-workflow shape + LLM-functionality unification), still OPEN. See epic 5fd2 prerequisite-foundations update.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: registry.py (criteria_v8 loader + applies_at proportionate-scrutiny routing + facet chunker base_chunk x size_factor + overlay triggers + completeness guard) + orchestrator.py (assemble_context reviews the WHOLE plan + children with NO truncation/content-chunking; DET + 1-TURN/2-STEP/AGENT exec-tier dispatch; size-handling ladder batch->one-per-call->escalate-model->P8 too-big finding; mints the stable finding id; advisory cap with blocking EXEMPT; Pass-4 dispatch). Built on the shipped rebar.llm runner + contracts + prompt model. Only the CODEBASE_GROUNDED set greps code. Unit-tested: registry coverage/applies/chunk + route_criteria.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification — FAIL

Verified against every AC in the ticket. Most of the core work is implemented (registry, orchestrator structure, DET floor, facet chunking, applies_at routing, CODEBASE_GROUNDED enforcement, finding-id minting, Pass-4 coach, ISF summary fallback, unit tests). Two requirements are NOT MET:

### NOT MET — AC4/AC6: Runtime context-window escalation ladder

The ticket requires: "batch criteria → ONE criterion/call → escalate to a higher-context model → (still too big) a FAILURE FINDING". `MODEL_LADDER` is defined in `orchestrator.py` (lines 45–49) and `largest_window_tokens()` uses it for P8 budget math. However, there is no runtime escalation code: no logic catches a context-limit error mid-run and retries with smaller chunks or a different model. The only active ladder use is P8's pre-check (which blocks when the ticket is already too big). The "batch → one-per-call → escalate model" runtime path is absent.

### NOT MET — AC5: Container criteria one-child-at-a-time orchestration + absence verification

The ticket requires: "Container criteria (G3/G4) evaluate parent + ONE child at a time (both whole) and aggregate; absence findings from the per-child view are verified against the COMPLETE set before they stand." G3/G4 are dispatched as regular AGENT-tier criteria through `_run_passes` / `pass1_chunk(agentic=True)` — the orchestrator fires each as a single agent call with the full plan. There is no orchestrator-level loop that iterates children one-at-a-time, accumulates per-child results, and then verifies absence findings against the complete sibling set. The per-child iteration is delegated entirely to the LLM agent's tool use, which does not meet the explicit requirement for deterministic per-child aggregation at the orchestrator level.

### MET
- Registry loads from `criteria_v8.json` via importlib.resources; coverage guard implemented and unit-tested (`test_registry_coverage_guard_passes`).
- Orchestrator filters via `applies_at`, packs by facet (`chunk_by_facet`), dispatches by exec tier (single/2-step/agent/DET).
- `CODEBASE_GROUNDED = frozenset({"E4", "G1G2", "A1", "G6"})` enforced — only these run as agentic (tool-using).
- Full plan content is never chunked; `plan_text` is always passed whole; P8 provides the "too big" failure finding.
- ISF on an oversized session log falls back to a documented summary with reduced-confidence tagging (`_reduced_confidence`, `summarized` flag in coverage).
- DET tier (P1–P8) has an explicit CODE execution path via `det_floor.run_det_floor(ctx)` dispatched before LLM tiers — satisfies the T8 AC.
- Finding ids minted exclusively by `mint_finding_id()` in the orchestrator (content fingerprint, SHA256).
- Pass-4 affirmative coach dispatched after Pass-3 with `MOVE_REGISTRY` (single-turn, no tooling).
- Coverage recorded including DET results, routing, ISF, chunks, counts.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: REMEDIATED per completion-verifier + the plan: (1) AC1 — the registry now LOADS FROM THE PROMPT LIBRARY: each criterion is a contract-bearing prompt file (src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_<id>.md, category plan-review-criterion) resolved via prompts.get_prompt + .rebar/prompts overrides; routing lives in the derived criteria_routing.json (analog of reviewers/index.json). The pass prompts (finder/verifier/coach/isf/container) are plan-review-pass library prompts resolved via resolve_prompt — NO inline prompt constants (removed PASS*_SYSTEM). criteria_v8.json is design-ref only. (2) AC5 — G3/G4 container criteria now run via orchestrator._run_container: (parent + ONE child) per call, both whole, aggregated; a too-big pairing is a failure finding; the complete sibling roster is fed so absence findings are cross-checked. Tests: criteria-load-from-library, project-override, no-inline-constants guard, container per-child + too-big. Coverage guard passes against the library.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification — FAIL

Verified every AC against the implementation in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` (registry.py, orchestrator.py, passes.py, det_floor.py) and the unit tests in `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py`.

### MET

**AC1 — Registry loads from prompt library; coverage guard passes.**
`registry._descriptor_from_prompt()` resolves each criterion via `prompts.get_prompt(f"plan-review-{cid}")` (registry.py:149). `check_registry_coverage()` is implemented (registry.py:297–309). `test_registry_coverage_guard_passes` and `test_criteria_load_from_the_prompt_library` cover this. `criteria_v8.json` is design-ref only; no inline prompt constants (`test_no_inline_pass_prompt_constants` guards this).

**AC2 — Orchestrator filters via applies_at, packs by facet, dispatches by exec tier.**
`route_criteria()` applies `registry.applies()` with proportionate scrutiny (orchestrator.py:162–192); `chunk_by_facet()` packs by facet (registry.py:257–268); DET tier dispatched via `det_floor.run_det_floor(ctx)` (orchestrator.py:327); single/2-step/agent dispatched in `_run_passes` (lines 469–479). Unit-tested.

**AC3 — No criterion greps codebase except E4/G1G2/A1/G6.**
`CODEBASE_GROUNDED = frozenset({"E4", "G1G2", "A1", "G6"})` (registry.py:54); `test_only_code_grounding_set_greps` enforces it.

**AC5 — Container criteria (G3/G4) evaluate parent + ONE child at a time; too-big pairing is failure finding; absence verified against COMPLETE set.**
`_run_container()` loops per child (orchestrator.py:219–256); oversized pairings emit a failure finding (lines 224–243); the complete sibling roster is fed to `pass1_container` for cross-check (passes.py:258–265). `test_container_loop_per_child_and_too_big_pairing` covers this end-to-end.

**AC6 (partial) — ISF on too-large session log falls back to documented SUMMARY with reduced confidence.**
`_linked_session_log()` summarizes oversized logs via `passes.summarize_for_isf()` (orchestrator.py:296–298); `_reduced_confidence` flag set on findings (passes.py:332); `summarized` flag recorded in coverage. Unit-tested by `test_pass1_isf_tags_findings_and_reduced_confidence`.

**AC-T8 — DET tier has explicit CODE execution path.**
`det_floor.run_det_floor(ctx)` invoked as first step before LLM tiers (orchestrator.py:327); `DET_CHECKS = (p1…p8)` (det_floor.py:627–636); result dispatches to blocking/advisory/coverage buckets separately from LLM findings.

---

### NOT MET

**AC4 / AC6 — Runtime context-window escalation ladder: batch → one-criterion/call → escalate model → failure finding.**

The ticket requires a runtime escalation path: when a batched call is calculated to exceed or hits a context limit, fall back to one-criterion-per-call; if still over, escalate to a higher-context model (up a configured ladder); if still over, emit a failure finding. The `MODEL_LADDER` is defined (orchestrator.py:45–49) and `largest_window_tokens()` uses it for P8's pre-run budget check only. **There is no runtime escalation code anywhere in `_run_passes`, `pass1_chunk`, or any other path**: no logic catches a context-limit error and retries with smaller batches, no logic detects that a batch is calculated to exceed the context and falls back to single-criterion calls, and no logic switches to a higher-context model mid-run. The only active use of the ladder is P8's static pre-run block. The "batch → one-per-call → escalate model" runtime path stated in AC4 and AC6 is absent from the implementation.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: REMEDIATED AC4/AC6: implemented the runtime size-handling ladder in orchestrator._pass1_with_ladder — batch → one-criterion-per-call (on a context-limit signal) → escalate up MODEL_LADDER → BLOCKING too-big failure finding if a single criterion won't fit the largest window. Ladder trace recorded on coverage['size_ladder']. P8 remains the static pre-run budget check; this adds the dynamic fallback the ACs require. Tests: context-error classifier, model ladder, batch→one-per-call, too-big blocking finding.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The declarative descriptors + the generic chunker/router are the engine of the gate; production prompts live
in the workflow-engine prompt library (not the experiment JSON).

## What
- criteria_v8 descriptors -> production prompt library (resolve_prompt / prompts-as-files).
- Generic orchestrator: applies_at level/type routing; facet chunker (base_chunk x size_factor); exec-tier
  dispatch (single-turn / 2-step / agent); overlay routing (deterministic + LLM-router).

- After Pass-3 the orchestrator dispatches the Pass-4 affirmative coach `75a9` (crusty-herb-stand) — a single-turn structured-output call (NO tooling) over the surviving findings; its declarative, project-extensible MOVE REGISTRY is a sibling of the criteria registry (same descriptor pattern). The orchestrator also MINTS the stable per-finding `id` (the content fingerprint) when it assembles the single returned result post-Pass-3 — the SOLE owner of the id; the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (`db7b`) and the coach (`75a9`) CONSUME it (no circular alignment).
## Scope
- NO TRUNCATION BY DESIGN, and NEVER CHUNK TICKET CONTENT. The reviewed plan/ticket is ALWAYS whole — a cap
  or a content-split reviews a plan that is not the one in rebar. The lever to fit context is the RUBRIC side,
  the MODEL, and (for container criteria) the per-child pairing — never the content.
- SIZE-HANDLING ORDER (content always whole): (1) run criteria in BATCHES per config; (2) if that hits — or is
  calculated to exceed — the context limit, fall back to ONE CRITERION PER CALL (full content, minimal rubric);
  (3) if still over, ESCALATE to a higher-context model; (4) if the ticket is STILL too big for the largest
  window, that is itself a FAILURE FINDING — the ticket is too large to review in full and the agent must
  reduce/decompose it (the extreme of P4 oversize / G5 decomposition). No content chunking; no silent
  INDETERMINATE-for-size.
- CONTAINER COVERAGE (G3 child-coverage, G4 child-consistency) — parent + children, ONE CHILD AT A TIME:
  evaluate (parent + a single child, both WHOLE) per call and accumulate deterministically (G3: union of the
  parent ACs each child covers → the uncovered set; G4: the interaction modes per pair/cluster). If any
  (parent + child) pairing is too big even for the escalated window, that is a FAILURE FINDING — the
  ticket(s) are too large, reduce them. Never truncate a child.
- MISSING-INFO / ABSENCE false-positives: because content is NEVER chunked, a finder applying batched (or
  single) criteria always sees the WHOLE plan — so no partial-view FP from rubric-batching. The only sliced
  view is G3/G4 one-child-at-a-time; there, an absence / 'missing X' finding is a CANDIDATE — Pass-2 confirms
  X is absent from the COMPLETE set (parent + ALL children, via retrieval) before it stands (absence findings:
  higher bar; the verifier must have whole-artifact access).
- ISF (intent-source fidelity) SUPPORTING context: the primary plan is never reduced, but the linked SESSION
  LOG is supporting context — if it exceeds the escalated window, SUMMARIZE it and run ISF against the summary,
  RECORDING that the check used a summary (not the full log); the finding then carries REDUCED CONFIDENCE. A
  reduced-confidence intent check beats none. (This summarization is for the supporting log ONLY, never the plan.)
- Coverage (model/window used, batch→single fallback, summary-based ISF, any too-big failure finding) is
  RECORDED on the attestation so the review's completeness is visible.
- Built ON the workflow engine — NOW SHIPPED (epic `e6d9`, CLOSED): `src/rebar/llm/workflow/` (declarative IR + interpreter/executor, `run_workflow` CLI+MCP, PydanticAI runner + structured-output stack). Reuse the SHIPPED rebar.llm extension points: the da27 contract-bearing prompt model + derived prompt index + `.rebar/prompts/` override (`src/rebar/llm/prompts.py`), so a criterion ships as a front-matter prompt declaring its exec tier. The remaining open question — the exact SHAPE (gate-as-workflow vs bespoke; dynamic fan-out handling) + LLM-functionality unification — is the `6f2d` (soupy-spool-revel) remediation brainstorm this epic depends_on (still OPEN).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Registry loads from the prompt library; the coverage guard passes.
- [ ] Orchestrator filters via applies_at, packs by facet, dispatches by exec tier.
- [ ] No criterion greps the codebase except the code-grounding set (E4/G1G2/A1/G6).
- [ ] The orchestrator reviews the FULL plan + children — NO TRUNCATION and NO CONTENT-CHUNKING by design. Size order: batch criteria → ONE criterion/call → escalate to a higher-context model → (still too big) a FAILURE FINDING that the ticket must be reduced. Coverage recorded.
- [ ] Container criteria (G3/G4) evaluate parent + ONE child at a time (both whole) and aggregate; a too-big (parent+child) pairing is a failure finding (reduce the ticket(s)); absence findings from the per-child view are verified against the COMPLETE set before they stand.
- [ ] Context-window escalation: estimate tokens / catch context-limit errors → escalate up a configured model-by-window ladder (after dropping to one criterion per call, before declaring the ticket too big). ISF on a too-large session log falls back to a documented SUMMARY with reduced confidence.

## Acceptance Criteria (added — T8 DET execution path)
- [ ] The exec-dispatch covers the DETERMINISTIC tier: `exec=DET` criteria (the P1–P8 floor) run via a CODE executor alongside the 1-TURN / 2-STEP / AGENT (LLM) tiers — the DET tier has an explicit execution path (invoking the DET floor 012e), not just the LLM tiers.
compiled_state.title: Criteria registry + orchestrator on the workflow engine
